text: Burnout

You should set a boundary with your inbox.
You ought to take real downtime each week.
You had better rest, or you will burn out.
You might want to try a mindful walk.
Welcome, Helen. So, tell me — how have you been sleeping lately?
Honestly, not well. We had two campaigns at once, and I feel close to burnout.
I hear that a lot. You should set a clear boundary with your inbox in the evening.
That is hard for me. If I stop checking, I feel I might miss something important.
I understand. But you had better protect your downtime, or the stress will follow you home.
You are right. I need to prioritize my health, not just the deadline.
Exactly. And you might want to start small — a ten-minute mindful walk after lunch.
I like that. A small habit to recharge — and to build some resilience.
That is the spirit. Rest is not a reward for the work — it is part of the work.
You had better protect your downtime.
You might want to take a mindful walk.
Let us prioritize health over the deadline.
Rest is part of the work, not a reward.

Welcome back to the show. Today, one simple shift: stop thinking about time, and start thinking about energy. Time is fixed — you always have twenty-four hours. But energy is a resource you manage. You can protect it or you can drain it. So here is my first recommendation: take a short, screen-free break every ninety minutes. Just five minutes. Stand up, breathe, look out a window. You should treat that pause as part of the work, not a reward for it. Do that for one week, and tell me how your afternoons feel.
